steps.title: Recognize vertex form
steps.content: The problem states the object reaches maximum height 27.04 feet at t = 1.3 seconds. This is the vertex. Using vertex form $f(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k$ where the vertex is $(h, k)$, we have $(h, k) = (1.3, 27.04)$. Therefore: $$f(t) = a(t - 1.3)^2 + 27.04$$

steps.title: Determine the sign of the leading coefficient
steps.content:
The object is launched from ground level and reaches a maximum before falling back down.

This means the parabola opens downward, so $a < 0$. Among the answer choices, we need the form with $(t - 1.3)^2$ (not $(t + 1.3)^2$) and a negative coefficient.

This gives us: $$f(t) = -16(t - 1.3)^2 + 27.04$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
